Marc: 	Anytime you are trying to prove a concept and that's what you are trying
to do here, everybody on this call probably has an offer that they want to
get out there. Anytime you want to test out a new offer, you always
would like to go with the Seed Launch. It's what I call the ‘proof of
concept launch’. It really tells us if your concept is going to work or not
and it tells you whether it's going to work or not quickly. The idea is to
really build up a list and I'd like to use the magic number of about 300
people. Then, put an offer in front of them. With the Seed Launch, we
don't have to get into these four video type of situations. We only have
to do really two videos if we are going to do videos at all. The first video
encompasses all the Prelaunch content and in the second video is your
sales video. You are making an offer in the sales video. You are sending
them to a buy button.
The goal here is to see if somebody is going to take out their credit card
and then actually purchase this from you. If you build up the list of 300
people and you get 20, 30, 40 people to buy, you hit the jackpot. You
prove the concept that people want to buy your offer or what you have.
This is great news. The other great news is if you put that offer out there
and you get no sales, this is great news and the reason it's great news is
